title: ABNF, Tokens, Whitespace, Line Endings, Limits, and Parser Architecture
description: A parser splits strings freely, accepts bare line feeds and whitespace before fields, has no byte limits, and mixes syntax recognition with application side effects. Learners cumulatively produce strict bounded incremental parser architecture through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.
Validate tokens and visible bytes using octet classes while rejecting separators, controls, NUL, and forbidden whitespace.
Require CRLF at HTTP/1.1 structural boundaries and document any local robustness exception without parser disagreement.
